summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the strip_html filter exhibits O(N^2) time complexity on crafted input with many '<' characters, causing event loop blocking proportional to the square of input length.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that the strip_html filter takes time proportional to N^2 where N is the number of '<' characters in the input, confirming the ReDoS vulnerability. The script will time the filter with increasing input sizes and verify quadratic scaling.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the reproduction image, installed LiquidJS from the target repo, and executed the timing script. The results confirmed O(N^2) scaling: doubling the input size caused roughly 4x increase in execution time.
verification_method: The verify.sh script runs exploit.js which: (1) applies strip_html to inputs of size N and 2N, (2) measures execution time for each, (3) verifies the ratio time(2N)/time(N) >= 3.0 (confirming quadratic or worse scaling). A linear-time implementation would produce a ratio of ~2.
evidence_produced_by_run: Timing measurements confirmed O(N^2) complexity: the ratio time(2N)/time(N) was greater than 3.0.
For N=40000, execution took ~379ms; for N=80000, execution took ~1516ms (ratio: 4.0).
The event loop was blocked during execution, confirming denial of service impact.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
